title: Market Brief — Tuesday, June 30, 2026
summary: GPU availability is loosening fast. Util-avail stands at 29% for A100, 40% for H100, 39% for H200, and 38% for B200, meaning no class is anywhere near shortage territory. The headline move is B200 spot surging 28.3% day-over-day to $5.94/hr, diverging sharply from H100 and H200 which both fell, signaling demand rotation into next-gen silicon even as overall utilization drops.
paragraphs: GPU availability is loosening fast. Util-avail stands at 29% for A100, 40% for H100, 39% for H200, and 38% for B200, meaning no class is anywhere near shortage territory. The headline move is B200 spot surging 28.3% day-over-day to $5.94/hr, diverging sharply from H100 and H200 which both fell, signaling demand rotation into next-gen silicon even as overall utilization drops.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), H100 inference runs roughly $7.31/M tokens and H200 hits $7.49/M for Llama 70B. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling far below production cost, burning cash to hold market share. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $5.94/hr with 123.9% annualized volatility on its nearest peer H200 looks overvalued against a backdrop of 38% available capacity and softening utilization across legacy chips. Undervalued trade: lock in H100 contracts at $2.00/hr median while utilization bleeds off at negative 21.4 percentage points over 30 days.
bullets: 40% of H100 compute capacity sits available per Ornn utilization data, the loosest GPU class tracked.
29% A100 util-avail marks it as the tightest class, yet still far from scarcity thresholds.
$5.94/hr B200 spot jumped 28.3% in one day while H200 fell 6.7%, a clear generational demand shift.
21.36 on the LLMflation Index means output token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.34/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss exceeding $4/M against TCO-adjusted H100 inference cost.
61.1% annualized volatility on H100 OCPI makes short-duration spot contracts the highest-risk-adjusted way to access compute.
